description: Acute stroke FAST positive in a 72-year-old woman within tPA window.

patient.name: Margaret L., 72F
patient.chief_complaint: Sudden onset facial droop on right side, slurred speech, and right arm weakness 25 minutes ago. Witnessed by spouse at onset. Cannot raise right arm to shoulder level.
patient.history: Atrial fibrillation on apixaban, HTN, hyperlipidemia

rubric_notes: Three FAST positives (Face, Arm, Speech) within the 4.5-hour tPA window. GCS 14 concerning (mild altered mental status). HTN at 175/95 typical of acute stroke. Per RUBRIC §3 stroke FAST positive mandates ESI 1-2. Time-critical: every minute of delay = 1.9M neurons lost. Atrial fib + apixaban = cardioembolic stroke until proven otherwise.
